reasoning: Leya Garifullina's Elo rating is 2453. A 1% increase equates to gaining roughly 25 Elo points, bringing her rating to ~2478. The time horizon is roughly 3 months (end of Oct 2025 to Jan 2026). As an established 21-year-old player with a rating > 2400, her K-factor is 10. To gain 25 rating points with K=10, she must exceed her expected score by roughly 2.5 points in official classical games. Professional players might play 2 to 3 classical tournaments (15-25 games) over a 3-month span. Across 20 games, the standard deviation of Elo change is about 22 points. Thus, gaining 25 points represents an overperformance of about 1.1 to 1.5 standard deviations. Taking into account the necessity of remaining highly active and significantly overperforming her current standing, the probability of reaching this threshold is roughly 11%. Therefore, a 1% gain over just a ~3-month window is a fairly low-probability event.
